title: Ubuntu: Please provide `apt` repository for obtaining latest podman version
body:
### Feature request description

Dear Developers,

Thank you for developing and maintaining podman.

```
Distributor ID:	Ubuntu
Description:	Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS
Release:	24.04
Codename:	noble
Linux 6.17.0-14-generic #14~24.04.1-Ubuntu SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu Jan 15 15:52:10 UTC 2 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```
The latest version of podman available through `apt` is 4.9.3 and v4.9 seems to be two years ago.

The steps to compile the latest GitHub release of podman from source are quite long and may run into issues.

It would be very helpful if an `apt` repository is provided, similar to [docker](https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/ubuntu/) and [nvidia-ctk](https://docs.nvidia.com/datacenter/cloud-native/container-toolkit/1.14.3/install-guide.html#installing-with-apt).


### Suggest potential solution

_No response_

### Have you considered any alternatives?

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: I believe @lsm5 had a PPA he was maintaining quite a while back on a best-effort basis. But I don't think we have any plans for setting up a PPA.

Hi @Luap99 and @lsm5 ,

Thank you for reviewing and for your comments.
I looked through https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/25219#discussioncomment-13168816 

As you very likely know, Apptainer provides pre-built binaries through GitHub and has Linux distro repository support:
https://apptainer.org/docs/admin/main/installation.html#install-from-pre-built-packages

Would it be possible to provide Podman pre-built binaries through GitHub?

There is no point in providing just podman binaries, podman is a dynamically linked binary by default, doing static builds is more involved and removes features so I would never recommend that. Besides it would not solve the countless of dependencies you still would need to install.

If you really want static binaries there are people building them, e.g. https://github.com/mgoltzsche/podman-static
